title: attribution — field classification
description: Attributions are behavioural marketing metadata (confidential — tenant-competitive). Touchpoints carry device + IP hash + consent snapshot (confidential + PII-adjacent — redacted on user erasure). Click IDs are third-party tokens (confidential — ad-network vendor lock-in signal). No health data, no financial account data on any owned row.

confidential: Attribution.first_touch_source (competitive info — which channels the tenant relies on)
Attribution.first_touch_device (jsonb — user-agent parsed; not raw fingerprint)
AttributionTouchpoint.device_snapshot (jsonb — includes hashed IP + parsed UA fields)
regulated_pii: AttributionTouchpoint.device_snapshot.ip_hash (SHA-256 hash of client IP — pseudonymised but still traceable to a household)
AttributionTouchpoint.device_snapshot.user_agent_hash (SHA-256 of full UA string — potential fingerprinting concern; hashed to prevent raw reads)
audit_log: Attribution.first_touch_source / campaign / medium (immutable post-create; attempted changes logged)
AttributionTouchpoint.consent_snapshot (regulator queryable — proof of consent at capture time)

notes: IP addresses are NEVER stored raw. Every touchpoint's device_snapshot.ip_hash is the SHA-256 of the client IP. Reversing the hash requires knowing the input space (IPv4 = 2^32; feasible for a nation-state, infeasible for opportunistic attackers).
User-Agent strings are hashed similarly for device_snapshot.user_agent_hash. The parsed fields (browser / OS / device_type / is_mobile) are stored in the clear because they don't uniquely identify.
Click IDs are confidential because they're third-party tokens — leaking them to competitors would allow reverse-engineering of ad-spend budgets + campaign taxonomy.
Landing page URLs are confidential — they reveal the tenant's URL structure + internal campaign codes (utm_content often carries A/B-test variant IDs).
consent_snapshot is confidential because it's a regulator-queryable proof-of-consent — visible to compliance staff + auditors, not to marketing operators.

The redactor pipeline strips device_snapshot.ip_hash + user_agent_hash on egress for callers who lack attribution.view-confidential permission. Marketing role sees the source/medium/campaign fields but not the PII-adjacent fields.

Reset (GDPR Art. 17) replaces every confidential field with '[REDACTED]' AND schedules touchpoint hard-delete 90 days out. The lifecycle_state transitions to 'churned' with subject_id NULL — row survives for aggregate metrics only.
